beats.narration_text: Bonjour. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of fermi's golden rule. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of fermi's golden rule

beats.narration_text: Fermi's Golden Rule is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. P(Ω, t) = |V|² · 4sin²(Ωt/2) / Ω²  |  ħ = 1 units  |  |V| = 0.1. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Fermi's Golden Rule. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Time  t, Density of states  ρ. Verify: P(Ω, t) = |V|² · 4sin²(Ωt/2) / Ω²  |  ħ = 1 units  |  |V| = 0.1. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Fermi's Golden Rule. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Time  t, Density of states  ρ. Verify: P(Ω, t) = |V

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from qmcg-fermis-golden-rule.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: fermi's golden rule. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag Time  t from 0.1 to 20. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of Time  t from 0.1 to 20

beats.narration_text: P(Ω, t) = |V|² · 4sin²(Ωt/2) / Ω²  |  ħ = 1 units  |  |V| = 0.1. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of fermi's golden rule. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Fermi's Golden Rule simulation and add a second slider that controls Time  t. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this Fermi's Golden Rule simulation and add a second slider that controls Time  t. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

beats.narration_text: Fermi's Golden Rule — Transition Probability. Medhavy.
beats.new_visual_element: Outro: Fermi's Golden Rule — Transition Probability · @Medhavy
